definition: The fertilizer supply chain is a structurally recurring geopolitical chokepoint. Russia + Belarus control ~40% global potash, Russia ~15% nitrogen, Morocco/OCP 70%+ phosphate rock. Russia's demographic-strategic feedback loop (declining population → power projection → conflict → supply disruption) makes this structurally recurring, not event-specific.
evidence: Russia + Belarus ~40% global potash, Russia ~15% nitrogen, Morocco/OCP 70%+ phosphate rock
2022 natural experiment: prices 3-4x, planting costs +30-50%, SSA yield decline 10-20%
Russia demographic-strategic feedback loop is structurally recurring
Morocco phosphate concentration adds a second geopolitical chokepoint
open_questions: Can Canadian potash and new phosphate mines reduce concentration on 5-10 year horizon?
How does Morocco phosphate compare to Russian energy as geopolitical lever?
What is price elasticity of fertilizer demand in developing economies?
